title: Revealing Dark Vessels in the Mauritius Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) Using Multi-Temporal SAR and AIS Data
one_line_takeaway: Multi-temporal SAR fused with AIS can flag vessels operating without AIS, giving maritime authorities a practical dark-vessel detection layer for MDA.
d4d_relevance: Directly supports a resilient maritime COP by showing how non-cooperative vessel detections from satellite SAR can preserve situational awareness when AIS is absent, spoofed, or denied. For a T3 semantic COP, the paper maps well to entity/event fusion: vessel detections, AIS identity gaps, protected-area incursions, and confidence-scored dark-vessel alerts.
methods_or_architecture: compare SAR-detected vessels against AIS-reported positions
use multi-temporal observations to monitor vessel activity patterns
fuse satellite and vessel-reporting data for maritime surveillance
apply geospatial analysis over EEZ and MPA areas
support AI-enabled satellite data fusion in the Nereus project
outputs_metrics: dark vessel candidates lacking AIS correlation
alerts or evidence for possible illegal fishing activity
no quantitative detection metrics provided in the available excerpt
prototype_hooks: create a semantic event type for SAR_DETECTION_WITHOUT_AIS
link AIS tracks, SAR detections, geofences, and vessel identities in a COP knowledge graph
show dark-vessel confidence scores when SAR detections cannot be matched to AIS
prioritize alerts inside EEZs, MPAs, convoy lanes, or exclusion zones
support disconnected operations by caching compact semantic alerts instead of raw imagery
use SAR-derived detections as an alternate sensing layer when AIS feeds are degraded or denied
pitch_evidence: UNDP Ocean Innovators selected the Nereus project to combat illegal and destructive fishing
the use case targets Small Island Developing States and Least Developed Countries, matching resource-constrained maritime security needs
the approach combines SAR, AIS, and VMS rather than relying on a single fragile data source
the Mauritius EEZ and MPAs provide a concrete operational setting for authority-facing MDA
limitations_risks: only abstract-level text was available, so implementation details are uncertain
no precision, recall, latency, revisit-rate, or false-alarm metrics are provided in the excerpt
SAR-to-AIS matching can be affected by timing mismatch, geolocation error, small-vessel detectability, and dense traffic
the paper does not appear to directly address denied communications, edge deployment, or tactical data dissemination
AIS and VMS gaps may reflect benign outages as well as deliberate concealment
